types.name: RunObject

fields.name: id
fields.description: The identifier, which can be referenced in API endpoints.

fields.name: object
fields.description: The object type, which is always `thread.run`.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run was created.

fields.name: thread_id
fields.description: The ID of the [thread](/docs/api-reference/threads) that was executed on as a part of this run.

fields.name: assistant_id
fields.description: The ID of the [assistant](/docs/api-reference/assistants) used for execution of this run.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The status of the run, which can be either `queued`, `in_progress`, `requires_action`, `cancelling`, `cancelled`, `failed`, `completed`, `incomplete`, or `expired`.

fields.name: required_action
fields.description: Details on the action required to continue the run. Will be `null` if no action is required.

fields.name: last_error
fields.description: The last error associated with this run. Will be `null` if there are no errors.

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run will expire.

fields.name: started_at
fields.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run was started.

fields.name: cancelled_at
fields.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run was cancelled.

fields.name: failed_at
fields.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run failed.

fields.name: completed_at
fields.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run was completed.

fields.name: incomplete_details
fields.description: Details on why the run is incomplete. Will be `null` if the run is not incomplete.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The model that the [assistant](/docs/api-reference/assistants) used for this run.

fields.name: instructions
fields.description: The instructions that the [assistant](/docs/api-reference/assistants) used for this run.

fields.name: tools
fields.description: The list of tools that the [assistant](/docs/api-reference/assistants) used for this run.

fields.name: temperature
fields.description: The sampling temperature used for this run. If not set, defaults to 1.

fields.name: top_p
fields.description: The nucleus sampling value used for this run. If not set, defaults to 1.

fields.name: max_prompt_tokens
fields.description: The maximum number of prompt tokens specified to have been used over the course of the run.

fields.name: max_completion_tokens
fields.description: The maximum number of completion tokens specified to have been used over the course of the run.

types.description: Represents an execution run on a [thread](/docs/api-reference/threads).

types.name: RunObjectStatus
types.description: The status of the run, which can be either `queued`, `in_progress`, `requires_action`, `cancelling`, `cancelled`, `failed`, `completed`, `incomplete`, or `expired`.

types.name: RunObjectRequiredAction

fields.name: type
fields.description: For now, this is always `submit_tool_outputs`.

fields.name: submit_tool_outputs
fields.description: Details on the tool outputs needed for this run to continue.

types.description: Details on the action required to continue the run. Will be `null` if no action is required.

types.name: RunObjectRequiredActionSubmitToolOutputs

fields.name: tool_calls
fields.description: A list of the relevant tool calls.

types.description: Details on the tool outputs needed for this run to continue.

types.name: RunToolCallObject

fields.name: id
fields.description: The ID of the tool call. This ID must be referenced when you submit the tool outputs in using the [Submit tool outputs to run](/docs/api-reference/runs/submitToolOutputs) endpoint.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of tool call the output is required for. For now, this is always `function`.

fields.name: function
fields.description: The function definition.

types.description: Tool call objects

types.name: RunToolCallObjectFunction

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function.

fields.name: arguments
fields.description: The arguments that the model expects you to pass to the function.

types.description: The function definition.

types.name: RunObjectLastError

fields.name: code
fields.description: One of `server_error`, `rate_limit_exceeded`, or `invalid_prompt`.

fields.name: message
fields.description: A human-readable description of the error.

types.description: The last error associated with this run. Will be `null` if there are no errors.

types.name: RunObjectLastErrorCode
types.description: One of `server_error`, `rate_limit_exceeded`, or `invalid_prompt`.

types.name: RunObjectIncompleteDetails

fields.name: reason
fields.description: The reason why the run is incomplete. This will point to which specific token limit was reached over the course of the run.

types.description: Details on why the run is incomplete. Will be `null` if the run is not incomplete.

types.name: RunObjectIncompleteDetailsReason
types.description: The reason why the run is incomplete. This will point to which specific token limit was reached over the course of the run.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of tool being defined: `code_interpreter`

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of tool being defined: `file_search`

fields.name: file_search
fields.description: Overrides for the file search tool.

types.name: AssistantToolsFileSearchFileSearch

fields.name: max_num_results
fields.description:
The maximum number of results the file search tool should output. The default is 20 for `gpt-4*` models and 5 for `gpt-3.5-turbo`. This number should be between 1 and 50 inclusive.

Note that the file search tool may output fewer than `max_num_results` results. See the [file search tool documentation](/docs/assistants/tools/file-search#customizing-file-search-settings) for more information.

types.description: Overrides for the file search tool.

types.name: FileSearchRankingOptions

fields.name: score_threshold
fields.description: The score threshold for the file search. All values must be a floating point number between 0 and 1.

types.description:
The ranking options for the file search. If not specified, the file search tool will use the `auto` ranker and a score_threshold of 0.

See the [file search tool documentation](/docs/assistants/tools/file-search#customizing-file-search-settings) for more information.

types.name: FileSearchRanker
types.description: The ranker to use for the file search. If not specified will use the `auto` ranker.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of tool being defined: `function`

fields.name: description
fields.description: A description of what the function does, used by the model to choose when and how to call the function.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function to be called. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

types.name: RunCompletionUsage

fields.name: completion_tokens
fields.description: Number of completion tokens used over the course of the run.

fields.name: prompt_tokens
fields.description: Number of prompt tokens used over the course of the run.

fields.name: total_tokens
fields.description: Total number of tokens used (prompt + completion).

types.description: Usage statistics related to the run. This value will be `null` if the run is not in a terminal state (i.e. `in_progress`, `queued`, etc.).

types.name: TruncationObject

fields.name: type
fields.description: The truncation strategy to use for the thread. The default is `auto`. If set to `last_messages`, the thread will be truncated to the n most recent messages in the thread. When set to `auto`, messages in the middle of the thread will be dropped to fit the context length of the model, `max_prompt_tokens`.

types.description: Controls for how a thread will be truncated prior to the run. Use this to control the initial context window of the run.

types.name: TruncationObjectType
types.description: The truncation strategy to use for the thread. The default is `auto`. If set to `last_messages`, the thread will be truncated to the n most recent messages in the thread. When set to `auto`, messages in the middle of the thread will be dropped to fit the context length of the model, `max_prompt_tokens`.

types.name: AssistantsApiToolChoiceOption
types.description:
Controls which (if any) tool is called by the model.
`none` means the model will not call any tools and instead generates a message.
`auto` is the default value and means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or more tools.
`required` means the model must call one or more tools before responding to the user.
Specifying a particular tool like `{"type": "file_search"}` or `{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "my_function"}}` forces the model to call that tool.

types.name: AssistantsApiToolChoiceOptionString
types.description: `none` means the model will not call any tools and instead generates a message. `auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or more tools. `required` means the model must call one or more tools before responding to the user.

types.name: AssistantsNamedToolChoice

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the tool. If type is `function`, the function name must be set

types.description: Specifies a tool the model should use. Use to force the model to call a specific tool.

types.name: AssistantsNamedToolChoiceType
types.description: The type of the tool. If type is `function`, the function name must be set

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function to call.

types.name: AssistantsApiResponseFormatOption
types.description:
Specifies the format that the model must output. Compatible with [GPT-4o](/docs/models#gpt-4o), [GPT-4 Turbo](/docs/models#gpt-4-turbo-and-gpt-4), and all GPT-3.5 Turbo models since `gpt-3.5-turbo-1106`.

Setting to `{ "type": "json_schema", "json_schema": {...} }` enables Structured Outputs which ensures the model will match your supplied JSON schema. Learn more in the [Structured Outputs guide](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

Setting to `{ "type": "json_object" }` enables JSON mode, which ensures the message the model generates is valid JSON.

**Important:** when using JSON mode, you **must** also instruct the model to produce JSON yourself via a system or user message. Without this, the model may generate an unending stream of whitespace until the generation reaches the token limit, resulting in a long-running and seemingly "stuck" request. Also note that the message content may be partially cut off if `finish_reason="length"`, which indicates the generation exceeded `max_tokens` or the conversation exceeded the max context length.

types.name: ResponseFormatText

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `text`.

types.description: Default response format. Used to generate text responses.

types.name: ResponseFormatJsonObject

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_object`.

types.description:
JSON object response format. An older method of generating JSON responses.
Using `json_schema` is recommended for models that support it. Note that the
model will not generate JSON without a system or user message instructing it
to do so.

types.name: ResponseFormatJsonSchema

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_schema`.

fields.name: json_schema
fields.description: Structured Outputs configuration options, including a JSON Schema.

types.description:
JSON Schema response format. Used to generate structured JSON responses.
Learn more about [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

types.name: ResponseFormatJsonSchemaJsonSchema

fields.name: description
fields.description:
A description of what the response format is for, used by the model to
determine how to respond in the format.

fields.name: name
fields.description:
The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain
underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

types.description: Structured Outputs configuration options, including a JSON Schema.

pathParams.name: thread_id
pathParams.description: The ID of the [thread](/docs/api-reference/threads) that was run.

pathParams.name: run_id
pathParams.description: The ID of the run to modify.
